chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 1
chunks.text: # 🧠 YOLOv11 Computer Vision Powered Search Application

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 2
chunks.text: A **Streamlit-based computer vision search engine** powered by **YOLOv11**, allowing users to process large image datasets, perform object detection, and **search images using intelligent filters** such as object class, count thresholds, and logical conditions (OR / AND).

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 4
chunks.text: ## 🚀 Features

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 5
chunks.text:
### 🔍 Intelligent Image Search
- Search images by detected object classes
- OR / AND logic for multi-class queries
- Optional object count thresholds per class

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 6
chunks.text:
### 📸 Visual Result Exploration
- Bounding box visualization
- Highlight matched classes
- Responsive image grid layout
- Modern card-style UI

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 7
chunks.text:
### ⚙️ Inference & Metadata Management
- Run YOLOv11 inference on image folders
- Save metadata as JSON
- Load previously processed metadata
- Avoids re-processing images unnecessarily

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 8
chunks.text:
### 🧠 User-Friendly UI
- Built using Streamlit
- Interactive filters & controls
- Real-time result updates

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 10
chunks.text: ## 🗂 Project Structure

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 12
chunks.text:
YOLO_IMAGE_SEARCH/
│
├── app.py # Streamlit application
├── README.md # Project documentation
│
├── configs/
│ └── default.yaml # Model & data configuration
│
├── src/
│ ├── inference.py # YOLOv11 inference logic
│ ├── utils.py # Metadata utilities
│ └── config.py # Config loader
│
├── data/
│ └── processed/ # Generated metadata
│
└── requirements.txt # Python dependencies

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 15
chunks.text: ## 🛠 Tech Stack

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 16
chunks.text:
- **Python 3.9+**
- **YOLOv11 (Ultralytics)**
- **Streamlit**
- **PyTorch**
- **Pillow (PIL)**
- **NumPy**

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 18
chunks.text: ## ⚙️ Installation

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 19
chunks.text:
### 1️⃣ Clone the repository
```

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 20
chunks.text:
git clone [https://github.com/AdarshSinghTomar768/Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application](https://github.com/AdarshSinghTomar768/Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application)
cd yolo-image-search

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 22
chunks.text:
### 2️⃣ Create and activate virtual environment
```

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 23
chunks.text:
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate # macOS / Linux
venv\Scripts\activate # Windows

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 25
chunks.text:
### 3️⃣ Install dependencies
```

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 26
chunks.text: pip install -r requirements.txt

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 29
chunks.text: ## ▶️ Run the Application

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 31
chunks.text: streamlit run app.py

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 33
chunks.text:
Open in browser:
```

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 34
chunks.text: [http://localhost:8501](http://localhost:8501)

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 37
chunks.text: ## 📂 How to Use

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 38
chunks.text:
### 🔹 Process New Images
1. Enter image directory path
2. Provide YOLOv11 model weights (`yolo11m.pt`)
3. Click **Start Inference**
4. Metadata is saved automatically

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 39
chunks.text:
### 🔹 Load Existing Metadata
1. Provide path to `metadata.json`
2. Instantly load results without re-running inference

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 41
chunks.text: ## 🔍 Search Options

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 42
chunks.text:
| Feature | Description |
|------|------------|
| OR Search | Match any selected object |
| AND Search | Match all selected objects |
| Count Threshold | Limit object count per class |
| Bounding Boxes | Toggle detection boxes |
| Highlight Matches | Emphasize matched classes |
| Grid Layout | Adjustable image grid |

chunks.text: ## 📁 Metadata Format (Example)

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 45
Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 47
chunks.text: ## ⚡ Performance Notes

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 48
chunks.text:
* Supports **CPU**, **Apple Silicon (MPS)**, and **CUDA**
* Metadata caching improves performance
* Suitable for large image datasets

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 50
chunks.text: ## 🚧 Future Improvements

chunks.title: Computer-Vision-Powered-Search-Application chunk 51
chunks.text:
* 🔍 Click-to-zoom image preview
* 📤 Export filtered results
* 🧠 Semantic search using embeddings
* 📊 Analytics dashboard

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 1
chunks.text:
---
title: Real World Task Sim
emoji: 🚀
colorFrom: indigo
colorTo: blue
sdk: docker
app_port: 8000
pinned: false
---

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 2
chunks.text: # Real-World Task Simulation Environment (OpenEnv)

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 3
chunks.text: A standardized, containerized environment for evaluating AI agents on real-world tasks. This environment implements the OpenEnv specification and provides three tasks with incremental difficulty.

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 4
chunks.text: ## Tasks & Difficulty

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 5
chunks.text:
1. **Email Triage (Easy)**: Classify a set of emails into categories: Work, Personal, or Spam.
2. **Data Cleaning (Medium)**: Clean a CSV file by normalizing date formats, removing duplicates, and handling missing values.
3. **Code Review (Hard)**: Identify and fix a common Python bug (mutable default argument) in a code snippet.

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 6
chunks.text: ## Action & Observation Spaces

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 7
chunks.text:
### Action Space (`RealWorldTaskAction`)
- `task_id`: The ID of the task being addressed.
- `action_type`: The operation being performed (e.g., "submit").
- `payload`: A dictionary containing task-specific results (e.g., `{ "predictions": { "1": "Work" } }`).

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 8
chunks.text:
### Observation Space (`RealWorldTaskObservation`)
- `current_task`: The ID of the active task.
- `prompt`: Instructions for the agent.
- `data`: The raw data for the task (JSON list, CSV string, or Python code).
- `done`: Boolean indicating if all tasks are complete.
- `reward`: Cumulative score (0.0 to 1.0 per task).
- `last_reward`: Detailed feedback and score for the previous action.

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 9
chunks.text:
## Reward Function
- **Incremental**: Agents receive rewards at each step (one step per task).
- **Programmatic Graders**: Success is measured by deterministic logic (regex for dates, exact match for categories, code analysis for fixes).
- **Penalties**: Incorrect task IDs or invalid payloads result in zero reward for that step.

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 10
chunks.text: ## Usage

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 11
chunks.text:
### Local Development
1. Install dependencies:
 ```bash
 pip install openenv-core pandas openai
 uv lock
 ```
2. Run the environment:
 ```bash
 python -m server.app
 ```

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 12
chunks.text:
### Baseline Inference
Run the baseline script to evaluate an agent (requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `HF_TOKEN`):
```bash
python inference.py
```

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 13
chunks.text:
### Deployment
Deploy to Hugging Face Spaces using the OpenEnv CLI:
```bash
openenv push
```

chunks.title: Meta-Hackathon chunk 14
chunks.text:
## Specification Compliance
Validated using `openenv validate`.
- Typed Pydantic models.
- Standard `reset()`, `step()`, `state()` interface.
- `openenv.yaml` manifest.
- Containerized execution via `Dockerfile`.

chunks.title: FixForge chunk 1
chunks.text:
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from api.routes import router

chunks.title: FixForge chunk 2
chunks.text: app = FastAPI()

chunks.title: FixForge chunk 3
chunks.text:
app.add_middleware(
 CORSMiddleware,
 allow_origins=["*"],
 allow_credentials=True,
 allow_methods=["*"],
 allow_headers=["*"],
)

chunks.title: FixForge chunk 4
chunks.text: app.include_router(router)
